notes: Eval 0 (low-scores-briefs): Skill now catches all 5 brief quality dimensions (abstract, over-specified, prescriptive dialogue, procedural goals, emotional arc). Baseline caught similar issues but missed domain vocabulary and script delegation.
Eval 1 (post-extraction-assessment): Both scored 5/5 again. Still non-discriminating for pass rate, but with-skill now adds broader brief quality analysis that iteration 1 missed.

Eval 2 (act2-coach-briefs): MAJOR IMPROVEMENT — skill went from 2/5 to 5/5. Coach mode now works: mentions working/hone/, offers interactive walkthrough with accept/modify/skip, builds correct --act 2 --coaching coach command. Baseline also scored 5/5 — it may have learned from CLAUDE.md.

ANALYST NOTE: The baseline on eval 2 scored 5/5 (up from 2/5 in iteration 1). This may be variance or the baseline agent reading CLAUDE.md/project docs. The skill's advantage on eval 2 is qualitative: it provides structured proposals with current/suggested/reasoning format, which is much more actionable than the baseline's question-based format.
